---
title: "Toronto Rush Hour Traffic Analysis"
subtitle: "INF2167 Project - Group 7"
author: 
  - Stella Gregorski
  - Yiying Qin
thanks: "Code and data are available at: [https://github.com/srg-uoft/INF2167_Group7_Project](https://github.com/srg-uoft/INF2167_Group7_Project)."
date: today
date-format: long
abstract: "abstract goes here"
format: pdf
number-sections: true
bibliography: INF2167_Project_References.bib
---

\newpage

```{r}
#| include: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

# set up our R environment for the report by importing all the libraries we will need
library(tidyverse)
library(opendatatoronto)
```

# Introduction

Welcome...

# Research Questions(s) & Hypothesis

This project will aim to address some research questions and preeminent assumptions about traffic patterns, as discussed below.

## Questions

Our research questions are...

## Hypotheses

Some hypotheses we have are...

# Relevant Literature

According to [@cite-tellingstorieswithdata]...

# Dataset & Methods

## Dataset

### Data Source

Dataset obtained from [@cite-traffic_data].

### Measurement

Our data was measured by...

### Data Structure

This is how our dataset is structured

### Key Variables

Key variables from our dataset are...

## Methods

Methods we plan to apply in our analysis are...

# Exploratory Data Analysis

## Overview

Data analysis was done using statistical programming language [@cite-R] and the Tidyverse library [@cite-tidyverse].

## Key Aspect 1

One key dataset aspect that relates to our variables of interest is...

## Key Aspect 2

Another key dataset aspect that relates to our variables of interest is...

## Key Aspect 3

Another key dataset aspect that relates to our variables of interest is...

# Next Steps

Report Presentation Adjustments

\newpage

# References

::: {#refs}
:::
